title: acanthus mollis
intro: Acanthus mollis, also known as bear's breech or musk thistle, is a flowering plant in the acanthus family, native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Asia. The plant's leaves, flowers, and roots have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Acanthus mollis include treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as relieving symptoms of colds and flu. Acanthus mollis can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in herbal creams and ointments to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
